question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint exploit the EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER (-1) special case in hasBlock(), where nextBlockNumber is set to blockRange.lowerEndpoint(), by returning a blockRange with a very high lowerEndpoint, causing the subscriber to attempt to fetch a block number far ahead of the actual chain tip and fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint exploit the early return condition in doGet() where blockNumber == EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER && !shouldGetBlock(nextBlockNumber.get()), by manipulating the block range returned to set nextBlockNumber beyond endBlockNumber, causing doGet() to silently return without processing any blocks and stalling the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint send a null response from grpcCall.read() immediately (simulating stream end without any data), causing the while loop to exit without processing any blocks and without calling onError(), effectively causing the subscriber to silently skip block processing for that node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: close()] Can an unprivileged external user who can trigger the close() method (e.g., via a crafted shutdown signal or by exploiting a lifecycle management vulnerability) cause Objects.requireNonNull(nodes.get()) to throw NullPointerException if nodes.get() returns null, crashing the close() method and leaving gRPC channels open and leaking resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the synchronized getBlockNodes() method and the AtomicReference nodes.set(newNodes) call, where a concurrent close() call reads the old node list while getBlockNodes() is replacing it, causing some nodes to be closed twice and others to remain open with stale channels?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence blockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() (e.g., by registering many nodes on-chain) cause getBlockNodes() to create a very large number of BlockNode instances, each opening a ManagedChannel, exhausting file descriptors or connection pool resources and causing >= 30% of existing nodes to fail to establish new connections?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onError()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint exploit the fact that readmitTime is set to Instant.now().plus(readmitDelay) (default 1 minute) after deactivation, and that tryReadmit(false) only reactivates if Instant.now().isAfter(readmitTime), by ensuring the node is deactivated just before a critical block needs to be processed, causing a 1-minute window where >= 30% of nodes are unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint send a very large number of small BlockItemSets (each below the maxBlockItems limit individually) that collectively cause the pending list in BlockAssembler to grow unboundedly in memory before an EndOfBlock is sent, causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint exploit the fact that the BlockAssembler.pending list is never size-limited in terms of number of BlockItemSet batches (only total item count via pendingCount), by sending many small batches each with a single item, causing the pending ArrayList to grow to 800,000 entries and consuming excessive heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlockRange()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint send a ServerStatusResponse with firstAvailableBlock=0 and lastAvailableBlock=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing getBlockRange() to return Range.closed(0, Long.MAX_VALUE), and then in streamBlocks() send blocks with numbers far beyond the actual chain tip, causing verification failures in BlockStreamVerifier.verifyBlockNumber() that propagate as exceptions and trigger onError()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: nodesChanged()] Can an unprivileged external user who can submit on-chain node registration transactions cause nodesChanged() to detect a property change (e.g., a port number change) for >= 30% of nodes, triggering currentNodes.forEach(BlockNode::close) which shuts down all their ManagedChannels, and then immediately submitting another change to prevent the new channels from stabilizing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on getBlockNodes() to cause a thread starvation attack, where a slow blockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() call (e.g., due to a slow database query triggered by auto-discovery) holds the monitor lock and blocks all other threads trying to call getBlockNodes(), stalling block processing across all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint send a SubscribeStreamResponse with BLOCK_ITEMS case containing a BlockItemSet where getBlockItemsList() returns a list with a null first element, causing a NullPointerException in onBlockItemSet() when calling blockItems.getFirst().getItemCase(), which is caught by the outer catch block and triggers onError()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onError()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint exploit the non-atomic check-then-act in onError() where errors.incrementAndGet() >= maxSubscribeAttempts is checked and then errors.set(0) is called, by racing concurrent calls to streamBlocks() on the same node to cause the error counter to be incremented past the threshold multiple times before being reset, causing the node to be deactivated prematurely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: tryReadmit()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node endpoint exploit the non-synchronized tryReadmit() method, where active is a plain boolean field (not volatile or atomic), causing a visibility issue where one thread sees active=true while another thread has already set it to false via onError(), leading to concurrent streamBlocks() calls on a node that should be inactive?
